columns.extraction_plan: The OS rate for the treatment arm (standard of care plus abiraterone) in the abiraterone trial is reported in the "Results" section on page 9. The OS rate for the treatment arm (standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide) in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial is not explicitly stated as a percentage in the text at a specific time point. Only median overall survival is provided.

The OS rate for the control arm (standard of care) in the abiraterone trial is reported in the "Results" section on page 9. The OS rate for the control arm (standard of care) in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial is not explicitly stated as a percentage in the text at a specific time point. Only median overall survival is provided.

The document does not report OS rates specifically for patients with synchronous metastasis in the treatment arm. The trials categorize patients by "metastatic disease" and "high-volume" or "low-volume" disease, but not by synchronous or metachronous status.

The document does not report OS rates specifically for patients with synchronous metastasis in the control arm. The trials categorize patients by "metastatic disease" and "high-volume" or "low-volume" disease, but not by synchronous or metachronous status.

The document does not report OS rates specifically for patients with metachronous metastasis in the treatment arm. The trials categorize patients by "metastatic disease" and "high-volume" or "low-volume" disease, but not by synchronous or metachronous status.

The document does not report OS rates specifically for patients with metachronous metastasis in the control arm. The trials categorize patients by "metastatic disease" and "high-volume" or "low-volume" disease, but not by synchronous or metachronous status.

The document reports median overall survival for the high-volume disease subgroup in Figure 3E on page 8, but does not explicitly state OS rates (percentages) at a specific follow-up time for the treatment arm.

The document reports median overall survival for the low-volume disease subgroup in Figure 3D on page 8, but does not explicitly state OS rates (percentages) at a specific follow-up time for the treatment arm.
